title: aspidium viscosum
intro: Aspidium viscosum, also known as the sticky maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipruritic effects. The health benefits of Aspidium viscosum include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis, as well as its use as a treatment for fever and rheumatism. Aspidium viscosum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
